source: Invest Like the Best — The cyclone under the surface (EP.260)
summary: AI has structurally changed cybersecurity, enabling scale advantages for the first time and allowing companies to break through the historical $15-20B market cap ceiling
summary_extended: Baker argues that AI has fundamentally reversed the structural dynamics of the cybersecurity industry, creating scale advantages where none previously existed. Historically, he was bearish on cybersecurity because attackers would optimize against any dominant player, VCs would fund competitors to exploit weaknesses, and larger scale actually hurt defenders rather than helped them — creating a natural market cap ceiling around $15-20B. AI-driven cybersecurity breaks this pattern because machine learning models improve with more data and more customers, meaning the largest players now compound their defensive advantage over time rather than becoming bigger targets. Baker points to the observable fact that cybersecurity companies have, for the first time, broken through the $20B market cap barrier and continued growing as empirical validation of this structural shift. The investment implication is to be structurally long the largest AI-native or AI-integrated cybersecurity platforms, as scale now confers durable competitive advantage rather than vulnerability.
detail: Baker was previously structurally bearish on cybersecurity because attackers would optimize against any dominant player, VCs would fund competitors, and scale hurt rather than helped. AI-driven cybersecurity reverses this: if models are learning, scale becomes an advantage. This is why, for the first time, cybersecurity companies have broken through the $20B market cap barrier and continue growing.
confidence_evidence: Baker describes this as a structural change in his view, contrasting with his prior bearish stance, and points to observable market cap outcomes as evidence.
quote_fragment: AI has changed that...scale is good for you...for the first time ever
